headline: Cyril Imhof's cycle centers on enterprise agentic AI adoption: slow, risky, and harder to build than conventional software.
body: Imhof returns repeatedly to why businesses hesitate on AI agents, citing security threats like prompt injection, data deletion, and unauthorized actions [0], technology immaturity comparable to early LLM stages [4], and the iterative, data-driven mindset that makes agents harder to build than traditional software [12]. He frames AI adoption as competitive necessity for enterprises facing widespread problems [8] and weighs infrastructure tradeoffs: local LLM deployment for privacy and control over cloud inference [7], avoiding self-built inference as too expensive and risky [9], and European cloud sovereignty against US giants [11]. He also covers the AI memory boom lifting Micron and SK Hynix on high bandwidth memory demand [3] and argues small agile teams outpace large ones because added developers bring communication overhead rather than speed [2][6]. Separately, Caleb Writes Code provides a visual walkthrough of GPT internals, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections [10].
